name: ML / AI service (training + inference)
summary: A service that trains statistical/ML models from data and serves predictions (batch or online) behind an API. Its behaviour is learned from data rather than fully specified, so correctness is defined by measured metrics on evaluation sets and remains valid only while the input distribution holds.

concerns: training/validation/test dataset provenance, lineage and versioning (immutable, reproducible snapshots)
label quality, inter-annotator agreement, and ground-truth definition; class balance and coverage of the target population
train/serve and train/test leakage prevention; temporal splits for time-dependent data
PII/sensitive attributes in training data: minimisation, consent basis, and de-identification
data governance: ownership, access controls, and licensing/usage rights of source data
questions: Are training datasets versioned and reproducible, with documented provenance and licensing?
Are label quality, class balance, and population coverage specified against acceptance thresholds?
Is leakage between train/serve and across splits explicitly prevented, and is PII in training data governed?

concerns: precise task definition (inputs, outputs, prediction target) and the operational design domain the model is valid within
offline evaluation sets and the metrics (accuracy, precision/recall, F1, AUC, calibration) with pass/fail thresholds
online evaluation (A/B, shadow, canary) and the business/quality metrics that gate promotion
explainability/interpretability requirements for predictions where decisions must be justified
handling of low-confidence, out-of-distribution, and abstain cases
questions: Is the prediction target defined with acceptance metrics and thresholds on a held-out evaluation set?
Are online-evaluation gates (shadow/canary/A-B) required before a model reaches production?
Are out-of-distribution and low-confidence inputs handled with a defined fallback or abstain behaviour?

concerns: inference latency (p95/p99) and throughput targets for the serving mode (online, streaming, batch)
GPU/accelerator VRAM and compute ceilings; batch-size, quantisation, and model-size tradeoffs
cost per prediction / per token and total serving cost envelope
training time, GPU-hour budget, and time-to-retrain
cold-start / model-load latency and autoscaling under bursty load
questions: Are inference latency (p95/p99) and throughput targets quantified for each serving mode?
Are GPU/VRAM limits and cost-per-prediction ceilings specified?
Are training-time and GPU-hour budgets bounded for the retraining cycle?

concerns: data and concept drift detection with defined metrics, thresholds, and alerting
retraining triggers (scheduled vs drift-triggered) and the automated retrain/validate/deploy loop
model rollback to a known-good version and champion/challenger safeguards
graceful degradation: fallback model, cached prediction, or rules-based path when serving fails
prediction-quality regression detection between model versions
questions: Is drift detection specified with metrics, thresholds, and an alerting/retraining response?
Can a bad model be rolled back to a known-good version, and is a serving fallback defined?
Are quality-regression checks required to block a degraded model from promotion?

concerns: reproducibility: pinned code, data snapshot, hyperparameters, seeds, and environment for every trained model
experiment tracking and a model registry recording metrics, lineage, and approval state
testing of a non-deterministic system: behavioural/invariant tests, metric-based CI gates, and eval harnesses
feature/transform parity between training and serving (single source of feature logic)
documentation via model cards / datasheets for accountability
questions: Can any deployed model be reproduced from pinned code, data, config, and environment?
Is a model registry required with lineage, metrics, and an approval/promotion state?
Are metric-gated CI tests and train/serve feature parity required?

concerns: adversarial robustness: evasion, model extraction, membership-inference, and data-poisoning defenses
prompt/model safety for generative models: jailbreak resistance, harmful-output filtering, output validation
protection of model artifacts and weights as intellectual property (access control, encryption)
authn/authz and rate limiting / abuse protection on inference endpoints
supply-chain integrity of pretrained/base models and third-party datasets
questions: Are adversarial threats (poisoning, extraction, evasion, membership inference) assessed and mitigated?
For generative models, are jailbreak resistance and harmful-output filtering required?
Are inference endpoints authenticated, rate-limited, and model artifacts access-controlled?

concerns: lawful basis and consent for using personal data in training; purpose limitation
GDPR Art. 22 automated decision-making: right to explanation, human review, and contestability
right to erasure of training data and its propagation into models (retraining / unlearning)
copyright and licensing of training corpora and generated outputs
bias/fairness obligations and discrimination risk for protected attributes; AI-regulation classification (e.g. EU AI Act risk tier)
questions: Is there a lawful basis for personal data used in training, and are automated-decision rights (explanation, human review) provided?
Is erasure of a subject's training data propagated into retrained models?
Are fairness/non-discrimination obligations and the applicable AI-regulation risk tier addressed?

concerns: production monitoring of model quality, drift, and prediction distributions (not just infra health)
serving infrastructure: GPU capacity planning, autoscaling, and multi-model / model-version routing
feature store / online-feature freshness and availability
the retraining/deployment pipeline as an operated system (schedules, approvals, runbooks)
ground-truth/feedback collection loop for delayed labels
questions: Is production monitoring of model quality and drift (beyond infra metrics) required?
Is GPU capacity, autoscaling, and version routing specified for serving?
Is a ground-truth/feedback collection loop defined for continuous evaluation?

concerns: fairness of outcomes across user subgroups (disparate impact, equalised odds) as an in-use property
calibrated user trust: surfacing uncertainty/confidence and avoiding over-reliance
real-world effectiveness of decisions vs the offline metric proxy
harm from false positives/negatives weighted by their real cost
questions: Are fairness metrics across user subgroups defined as acceptance criteria?
Are per-class error costs (false-positive vs false-negative harm) weighted in the success definition?

concerns: harm from confidently wrong predictions in consequential decisions (health, finance, safety functions)
human-in-the-loop / human oversight for high-stakes automated decisions
guardrails and safe-default behaviour when the model is uncertain or unavailable
toxic/unsafe generative output and its downstream real-world consequences
questions: For consequential decisions, is human oversight or a safe-default required when confidence is low?
Are guardrails specified for harmful, unsafe, or out-of-domain outputs?
